faction_indicators.color_identity: Uniform color variant (C designation, same as JP Carp)
Uniform color variant (D designation, same as JP Dragons)
Uniform color variant (R designation, same as JP Swallows)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Status box displays 'IN.TOP' (JP: 回オモテ Top of inning)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player team goes to field, opponent team at bat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Status box displays 'IN.BOT' (JP: 回ウラ Bottom of inning)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player team goes to bat, opponent team goes to field

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Team initial letters change based on region (JP NPB vs US MLB teams)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different team rosters and statistics available by region

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 6 Japanese teams or 6 US teams displays distinct uniform colors to establish team identity; pairs G&A and S&R share color similarity within Japanese and US contexts respectively
Teams identified by single letter (C, D, G, S, T, W for Japan; A, C, D, P, R, Y for US) creating visual/textual identity without licensed names
Status box text changes from Japanese kanji/hiragana to English abbreviations, communicating regional context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Team enters batter's box; opponents position on field
Team distributes across field positions; opponents prepare to bat

notes: Baseball (NES/Famicom) is a foundational sports title with minimal narrative or political world-state complexity. Faction differentiation is purely aesthetic team identity through uniform colors and initial letters. No danger/safety signals exist; game is purely competitive sport simulation. Regional differences reflect localization strategy rather than world-state variation. The game lacks environmental storytelling beyond team visual identity. Data tier T3 reflects limited documentation on visual manifestation details.
